methodology_config.global_instructions:
You are generating artifacts for a Waterfall methodology project. Waterfall follows sequential phases: Requirements → Design → Implementation → Testing → Deployment.
Rules: - Use formal language: "The system shall..." for all requirements - Every requirement must be atomic — one behavior per requirement - Traceability is critical — every artifact must reference its source - Prioritize completeness over speed — cover every SOW item - Flag any ambiguity as an assumption or risk - Do NOT use Agile terminology (no sprints, stories, epics, story points)

artifact_overrides.module_extraction: Group the SOW scope into major Functional Areas or Sub-systems typical of a formal Business Requirements Document (BRD). Each module should map to a chapter or section in the BRD.
artifact_overrides.functional_req: - Use formal requirement statement format: "The system shall [action] when [condition]." - Each requirement must be atomic — one testable behavior per requirement - Assign priority: Critical, High, Medium, or Low - Include traceability: reference the source SOW section for every requirement - Group requirements by functional area
artifact_overrides.nonfunctional_req: - Use formal statement format: "The system shall [meet criteria] under [condition]." - Every non-functional requirement must have a measurable acceptance threshold - Include performance benchmarks, security standards, and compliance requirements - Reference industry standards where applicable (ISO, OWASP, WCAG)
artifact_overrides.task: - Break down into formal Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) tasks - Each task should map to a phase: Requirements, Design, Implementation, Testing, Deployment - Include effort estimates in hours - Do NOT use Agile terminology — no sprints, stories, or story points - Tasks should be sequential, not iterative
artifact_overrides.test_case: - Write formal test procedures suitable for a Test Plan document - Include detailed preconditions, step-by-step procedures, and expected results - Cover positive tests, negative tests, and boundary conditions - Each test case must trace back to a specific functional requirement - Include test environment requirements in preconditions
artifact_overrides.architecture: - Produce a formal architecture specification - Include component responsibilities, interfaces, and data flows - Reference specific technologies from the SOW context - Identify integration points and external dependencies - Include deployment architecture considerations
artifact_overrides.risk_entry: - Produce a formal Risk Register suitable for project governance - Include probability and impact ratings: High, Medium, Low - Every risk must have a concrete mitigation plan and contingency plan - Assign a risk owner role - Consider risks across all Waterfall phases
